categories.name: Insufficient Evidence
categories.definition: Cases where the PR diff/title/body and available evidence in this catalog are insufficient to confidently determine the root cause of the flakiness. Use this as a semantic fallback instead of guessing; should be revisited when more evidence is available.
symptoms: PR description/evidence is vague or missing
Patch changes do not clearly relate to the stated flaky behavior
common_triggers: Backlog evidence is low quality or mismatched with patch
Fix is a broad refactor without clear flaky signal
fix_patterns: Increase timeout / add retry without analysis
Add logging/assertions without pinpointing root cause

categories.name: Concurrency / Data Race
categories.definition: Flakiness caused by concurrent access to shared state without proper synchronization, leading to race conditions. Common in tests involving parallel execution, background goroutines, or shared test fixtures.
common_triggers: Parallel test execution (t.Parallel()) with shared state
Test isolation failures between test cases
fix_patterns: Remove t.Parallel() or isolate shared state
Proper cleanup and reset of global state
Migrate to testify with proper test isolation

categories.name: Async / Timing / Eventual Consistency
categories.definition: Flakiness caused by asynchronous operations, timing-dependent logic, or insufficient waiting for eventual consistency. Includes issues with time.Sleep(), timeouts, and async task completion.

categories.name: Shared State / Global State Pollution
categories.definition: Flakiness caused by tests polluting shared or global state that affects subsequent tests. Includes global variable modifications, shared database tables, and test isolation failures.
symptoms: Test passes in isolation but fails in suite
common_triggers: Shared tables not cleaned between tests
fix_patterns: Use unique table names per test

categories.name: Non-deterministic Result Order
categories.definition: Test failures caused by assuming implicit ordering of results without explicit ORDER BY clause, or relying on container/map iteration order which is not guaranteed in Go. This is a common source of flakiness in TiDB tests.
symptoms: Query results match in content but fail in order
Flakiness in SELECT without ORDER BY
common_triggers: Missing ORDER BY in SELECT queries
Using map[string]X and assuming iteration order
fix_patterns: Add explicit ORDER BY in queries
Use ordered containers or sort before comparison

categories.name: Non-deterministic Plan Selection
categories.definition: Flakiness caused by optimizer choosing different execution plans across runs, often due to statistics variations, tie-breaking behavior, or plan cache issues. Test assertions on plan structure or cost may fail intermittently.
symptoms: Plan ID or cost assertion failures
common_triggers: Bind info or plan guide changes
fix_patterns: Use plan hints to stabilize plan
Avoid asserting on exact plan IDs

categories.name: Test Infrastructure Migration
categories.definition: Flakiness arising from migration between testing frameworks (e.g., old check suite to testify), test suite refactoring, or changes to test helpers/fixtures. Often manifests as test setup/teardown issues.
fix_patterns: Update test helpers for new framework

categories.name: External Dependency / Environment
categories.definition: Flakiness caused by dependencies on external systems (TiKV, PD, network) or environment-specific factors. Includes real TiKV tests, network-dependent tests, and resource availability issues.
fix_patterns: Use mock/store instead of real TiKV when possible
Add retry logic for network operations
Increase resource limits or handle gracefully

categories.name: Schema / DDL Change Race
categories.definition: Flakiness related to DDL operations and schema changes, including timing issues between schema update and subsequent queries, schema version synchronization, or metadata caching problems.
common_triggers: DDL execution not waited for completion
fix_patterns: Wait for DDL completion before querying
Use serial test suite for DDL tests

categories.name: Non-deterministic Test Input / Probabilistic Assertion
categories.definition: Flakiness caused by tests using randomized or non-deterministic inputs, or by assertions that rely on probabilistic properties rather than deterministic invariants.
symptoms: Rare failures only on some seeds/inputs
Random input generation triggers edge-case parsing/formatting
common_triggers: Using math/rand without a fixed seed
Probabilistic assertions (e.g., assuming random bits are never zero)
Random float/string formatting hitting scientific notation or rounding edge cases
fix_patterns: Use a fixed seed or per-test rand.Source and log the seed on failure
Avoid probabilistic assertions; assert deterministic invariants or accept equivalent outcomes

categories.name: Unclassified (Needs triage)
categories.definition: Temporary bucket for cases whose root cause is not yet confidently classified. Cases should be re-triaged later and moved to a specific category once enough evidence exists.
